rationale: PR delivers what the body claims across 11 files. supabase/dealer-leads.sql and RUN_IN_SUPABASE.sql add the lead_tasks table with id/lead_id/title/due_date/completed columns, two indexes (lead_id+due_date, completed), and RLS policies scoped through dealer_leads.dealer_id = auth.uid(). supabase/test-drive-bookings.sql adds the test_drive_bookings table with vehicle_id/dealer_id/customer_name/booking_time/status. dealer-crm-sample-leads.ts extends DealerCrmLead with optional schedule[] and tasks[] fields and populates the John Smith / Toyota Hilux lead with the exact three tasks ('Call customer tomorrow', 'Send finance quote', 'Arrange inspection') and Monday/Tuesday schedule. dealer-calendar-events.ts exports buildDealerCalendarIcs(), buildGoogleCalendarUrl(), getDealerCalendarEvents(), getNextDealerCalendarEvent(). /api/dealers/calendar/ics returns text/calendar with attachment headers; /api/dealers/calendar/reminders authenticates via supabase server cookies, looks up next event, sends push via sendPushToUser, and inserts an in-app notification. /dealers/calendar page groups events by weekday with Google Calendar links and the ICS download. The empty `catch {}` block in reminders/route.ts wraps insertNotification() with a documented comment ('Push is the primary action for this endpoint; in-app notification is best effort.') and the catch in DealerCalendarReminderButton.tsx sets a user-visible status message — both are intentional error handling, not error-swallow cheats hiding real bugs. error-swallow detector firing is a false positive.
